findings.ruleDescription: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.
findings.reportTitle: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation
findings.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

matches.description: Lines exceeding 3000 chars in source code suggest packed/obfuscated content

fullDescription.text: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.

message.text: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation

properties.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects common JavaScript obfuscation patterns used in supply chain attacks. Includes character permutation functions, hex escape sequences, String.fromCharCode encoding, and self-executing anonymous function wrappers typical of malicious payloads.
findings.recommendation: Do not execute this code. Deobfuscate the payload in a sandbox to understand its behavior. Check if the obfuscated code was part of the original project or introduced by a later contributor.

matches.description: Multiple hex escape sequences in string literals

fullDescription.text: Detects common JavaScript obfuscation patterns used in supply chain attacks. Includes character permutation functions, hex escape sequences, String.fromCharCode encoding, and self-executing anonymous function wrappers typical of malicious payloads.

matchDetails.description: Multiple hex escape sequences in string literals

properties.recommendation: Do not execute this code. Deobfuscate the payload in a sandbox to understand its behavior. Check if the obfuscated code was part of the original project or introduced by a later contributor.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects shell metacharacters embedded in URL strings, endpoints, or authorization URLs. CVE-2025-6514 exploited this by placing $(calc.exe) in an authorization_endpoint URL that was passed to the OS shell via the 'open' package. Also covers backtick and semicolon injection in URLs.
findings.reportTitle: CRITICAL: Shell metacharacters in URL — possible RCE
findings.recommendation: This code contains shell metacharacters ($(), backticks, semicolons, pipes) inside URL or endpoint strings. This is the exact pattern used in CVE-2025-6514 (mcp-remote RCE) where a malicious authorization_endpoint URL was passed to the OS shell. Never pass untrusted URLs to shell commands or the 'open' package.

fullDescription.text: Detects shell metacharacters embedded in URL strings, endpoints, or authorization URLs. CVE-2025-6514 exploited this by placing $(calc.exe) in an authorization_endpoint URL that was passed to the OS shell via the 'open' package. Also covers backtick and semicolon injection in URLs.

message.text: CRITICAL: Shell metacharacters in URL — possible RCE

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type

rules.name: File size anomaly for file type
